evals.prompt: My dashboard page is slow — it takes 5 seconds to load. I want to debug performance using Chrome DevTools. What should I measure, and how do I capture evidence?
evals.expected_output: Run Pattern 1 (Page Load Performance): navigate_page to the dashboard, performance_start_trace (with reload=true), wait for auto-stop, analyze insights for LCP (Largest Contentful Paint), INP (Interaction to Next Paint), CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift). Save trace to e2e-evidence/devtools-perf/step-01-trace.json.gz. Identify slowest phase (e.g., 'LCP delayed by slow API call'). Evidence: trace file + screenshot before and after load.

evals.prompt: A user says 'the sign-up form doesn't work'. I see the form on the page, but nothing happens when they submit. How do I debug this with DevTools?
evals.expected_output: Run Pattern 2 (Network Debugging): navigate_page to sign-up form, fill and submit the form, list_network_requests to see all API calls. Inspect the form submission request (POST to /api/signup) — check status code (should be 201), response body (should have user ID or token). Common bugs: (1) request not sent (form validation blocking), (2) request sent but fails (500 error). Get detailed request (get_network_request) and save to e2e-evidence/devtools-network/.
